channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Labor to allow conscience vote on telehealth for assisted dying amid elder abuse concerns
item.description: Labor has proposed removing legal barriers to telehealth consultations for assisted dying, sparking debate over balancing access and safeguards. The party's national conference approved a conscience v...

item.title: Geography and Development in Kenya’s 47 Counties: An Interview with a Scholar
item.description: Kenya's 47 counties, established under the 2010 Constitution to support devolution, are shaped by historical administrative units, population distribution, cultural identities, and physical landscapes...

item.title: Industry Leaders Showcase Cutting-Edge 9.7.4 Theater Audio System at Audio Advice Live 2026
item.description: At Audio Advice Live 2026, Ascendo Christie and Trinnov will unveil a groundbreaking 9.7.4 theater audio demo that redefines immersive sound experiences. This collaboration between two leading audio t...

item.title: National School of the Arts showcases emerging jazz talent through youth festival
item.description: The National School of the Arts (NSA) is collaborating with the Standard Bank Joy of Jazz Festival to host Jazz for Young People (JY4P), a youth-led event celebrating South Africa’s emerging jazz tale...

item.title: ASIS pushes for sustainable financing to boost Africa's economic growth
item.description: The Africa Social Impact Summit (ASIS) emphasized the need for innovative financing frameworks to strengthen resilience and transform Africa's economies. Keynote speaker Banji Oyelaran-Oyeyinka highli...

item.title: Rappler Fact-Check Debunks Claim of NBI Witness Arrest Post-Vice President's Impeachment Trial
item.description: The article clarifies that Senior National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) agent John Mark Calilung, who testified as the first witness in Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, has not been a...

item.title: US Condemns Killing of Dachomo Family Members, Calls for Justice
item.description: The United States government has strongly condemned the killing of family members of Rev. Ezekiel Dachomo in Plateau State, Nigeria. The Bureau of African Affairs issued a statement calling the attack...

item.title: Tanzania Faces Political Crackdown Amid Saba Saba Protests
item.description: Twenty-three individuals were charged with terrorism in Mwanza, Tanzania, on July 23, 2026, for their alleged involvement in the July 7 Saba Saba protests. The charges come amid a broader government c...

item.title: Nigerian Artists Release Fresh Music This Weekend
item.description: This week's Nigerian music releases spotlight eight fresh tracks across Afrobeats and street pop genres. The article highlights standout songs like Teni ft Chike's romantic 'Wurumanya' and Ayo Maff's ...

item.title: Bombay High Court sets up special courts to expedite paper leak case trials
item.description: The Bombay High Court has established two special courts in Aurangabad and Nagpur to expedite trials in examination paper leak cases across Maharashtra. This decision follows intense student protests ...

item.title: Guardian Readers' Responses to Analysis of Modern Rudeness
item.description: The article discusses a recent analysis of modern rudeness that sparked a wave of critical responses from Guardian readers. The piece highlights how the examination of contemporary manners has provoke...

item.title: Edmonton Elks secure thrilling 36-34 victory over Saskatchewan Roughriders with last-minute field goal
item.description: Vincent Blanchard’s 57-yard field goal on the final play of the game secured a dramatic 36-34 victory for the Edmonton Elks over the Saskatchewan Roughriders. The Elks, led by quarterback Cody Fajardo...

item.title: Safaricom Automatically Refunds Archived M-Pesa Balances Linked to Dormant Mobile Numbers
item.description: Safaricom has started an automated exercise to refund archived M-Pesa balances belonging to customers who previously used dormant or recycled mobile numbers. Thousands of customers across Kenya have r...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari Discusses His Luxury Car Collection and Safety Features
item.description: Pastor Victor Kanyari, leader of Salvation Healing Ministry, shared details about his car collection during a church service. He revealed owning four brand-new Toyota Land Cruiser V8s and plans to buy...

item.title: Suri Cruise's Acting Debut in Shakespeare Play: Following in Parents' Footsteps
item.description: Suri Cruise, 20, is set to make her acting debut in a contemporary adaptation of Shakespeare's 'A Midsummer Night's Dream' titled 'Midsummer!'. The young actress, who is majoring in musical theater at...

item.title: Charli XCX's 'Music, Fashion, Film' Explores Identity Instability Through Artistic Evolution
item.description: Charli XCX's latest project, 'Music, Fashion, Film,' delves into the complexities of identity and self-definition through a blend of music, fashion collaborations, and cinematic elements. Following he...

item.title: Madison Police Shooting Lacks Body Camera Footage, Sparks Debate on Accountability
item.description: The fatal police shooting of Corey Ruiz in Madison, Wisconsin, has highlighted the city's lack of body camera requirements for officers, leaving critical moments of the incident without official foota...

item.title: Nigeria's Largest Methamphetamine Bust: Court Inspects Jungle Lab Amid Drug Trafficking Concerns
item.description: A Nigerian court conducted a visit to a clandestine methamphetamine laboratory in Ogun State, which is at the heart of the country's largest drug seizure. The site, which was raided in May 2026, was e...

item.title: Zanzibar invests in early childhood education
item.description: The article discusses Zanzibar's commitment to early childhood education, highlighting recent investments aimed at improving developmental outcomes for young children. Published on July 24, 2026, the ...

item.title: Philippine Vessels Continue Mission Near Panatag Despite China's Actions
